title: hibbertia subvaginata
intro: Hibbertia subvaginata, also known as the scrub guinea flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dilleniaceae. Native to Australia, this plant has been used by traditional healers for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Hibbertia subvaginata include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or poultice for its medicinal uses, making it a valuable resource in traditional herbalism.
